steps.title: Paediatric shock
steps.text: A child in shock has received appropriate fluid resuscitation and treatment for sepsis but remains hypotensive. What is the next best step at the tertiary care level?
steps.rationale: Refractory shock despite adequate treatment requires consultation and escalation to vasoactive agents like adrenaline or noradrenaline.

textChoices.text: Discuss with a consultant and consider vasoactive agents

steps.title: Paediatric shock
steps.text: Which of the following is not a standard tertiary-level intervention for refractory paediatric shock?
steps.rationale: High-dose paracetamol is not part of the management for refractory shock. Vasoactives, corticosteroids, bicarbonate, and ICU admission are appropriate.

steps.title: Snake bite
steps.text: Which of the following is an appropriate first-line medication for pain management in a child with a snake bite?
steps.rationale: Oral paracetamol is the first-line treatment for pain. Morphine may be added if pain is severe.

steps.title: Snake bite
steps.text: When should limb elevation and monitoring for compartment syndrome be considered?
steps.rationale: Elevate the limb in cases of extensive swelling and monitor for compartment syndrome, which may require surgical intervention.

textChoices.text: Only for snake bites to the face
If the bite is more than 6 hours old

steps.title: Snake bite
steps.text: Which of the following is not a clinical indication for anti-snake venom administration?
steps.rationale: Mild local swelling alone is not an indication. Anti-venom is indicated for systemic signs or extensive swelling.

steps.title: Snake bite
steps.text: A child with a snake bite develops rapidly progressive swelling and cardiovascular instability. What is the most appropriate next step?
steps.rationale: These are clear indications for anti-snake venom. Anaphylaxis risk warrants readiness with adrenaline.

textChoices.text: Administer anti-snake venom with adrenaline standing by

steps.title: Snake bite
steps.text: What is the appropriate management when necrotic tissue is identified at the bite site?
steps.rationale: Surgical debridement is vital when necrotic tissue is present to prevent further complications.

steps.title: Toxicology
steps.text: When should endotracheal intubation be considered in a child with poisoning?
steps.rationale: Intubation and ventilation are indicated for airway protection in neurologically deteriorating children.

textChoices.text: In all cases of suspected ingestion

steps.title: Toxicology
steps.text: What is a key responsibility of the tertiary care team after initial stabilisation in a toxicology case?
steps.rationale: Once the patient is stabilised, the tertiary team should initiate condition-specific treatment.

textChoices.text: Begin definitive treatment for the specific poison
